sources.title: RSA 281-A:2 — Workers' Compensation Definitions (NH General Court)
sources.note: Confirmed 7 sub-criteria (A–G) in VI(b)(1). Primary statutory source.

sources.title: RSA 282-A:9 — Employment Status, ABC Test (NH General Court)
sources.note: Confirmed 3-part ABC test for UI. Primary statutory source.

sources.title: NH Business Review — New criteria used to determine independent contractor status
sources.note: Confirms 7-criteria test replaced 12-part test effective 6 August 2012 (2012 HB 1278).

sources.title: McLane Middleton — Federal IC Rules May Be Changing, NH Still Stricter
BeAuditSecure — NH State-Specific Employment Relationship Laws
sources.note: Confirms 7-factor test; lists all seven criteria.

sources.title: ALFA International — NH Workers' Compensation Compendium
sources.note: References 12-criteria language — this reflects pre-2012 law and is superseded.

sources.title: NH Business Review — NH Misclassification Fines (2019)

notes: Programmatic US-State child page. Compliance cache correctly described the dual-test structure (RSA 281-A:2 workers' comp + RSA 282-A:9 ABC UI) but incorrectly described the workers' comp test as '12-Criteria'. Current statute (RSA 281-A:2, VI(b)(1)(A–G)) has 7 criteria since August 2012 amendment. Row prompt reference to SB 217 (2024) with construction-specific penalties and HB 1278 (2024 session) could not be verified — NH SB 217 in 2024 was an educator incentive bill; HB 1278 in 2012 is confirmed (replaced 12 with 7 criteria) but a 2024 HB 1278 on worker classification was not found. Both flagged in HIGH_CONCERN.md. Page uses verified 7-criteria structure and does not reference unconfirmed 2024 bills.
